Starts off really fun. There are some nice ideas here and the visual style is unique and well done. The gameplay is fun... for a while.  There are only so many times it can be fun to have you walk into a new screen, get boxed in and have to fight your way out of a sardine tin. The controls are fine early on but once you add the later powerups they show just how clumsy they are... which wouldn'tt be so bad if many mandatory areas are designed with an almost "Kaizo Mario" mindset, exacerbatting the ♥♥♥♥♥♥ control scheme.By thet final stage I it had gottten so boring I used Cheat Engine to have invulnerability and infinite double jump, and it still felt like a chore to get to the end... only to get a long unskippable endgame sequence and an invitation to now try "Hard Mode". No thanks, it was already hard enough to sit through the end of normal mode.  A lot of care and thought clearly wentt into most of this game, but it feels like after a while the designers got just as bored as how it makes the player.

Guacamelee! Super Turbo Championship Edition is one of the most fun and charming metroidvanias out there. The game mixes fast-paced combat, platforming, wrestling moves, and Mexican folklore into something that feels incredibly unique and full of personality. From the moment you start uppercutting skeletons into the air while mariachi music blasts in the background, it’s hard not to smile.  The combat is easily one of the best parts of the game. Instead of basic button mashing, fights focus heavily on combos, dodging, juggling enemies, and using different special moves to break shields and control crowds. Once you unlock more abilities, combat becomes surprisingly satisfying and fluid.  The dimension-switching mechanic also keeps both combat and platforming interesting. Swapping between the world of the living and the dead mid-jump or during fights adds a lot of variety and creates some really creative platforming sections later on.  The humor deserves credit too. The game is full of dumb jokes, pop culture references, and over-the-top characters, but it never becomes annoying.  The good: - Fantastic art style and presentation - Extremely fun combat system - Great mix of platforming and fighting - Charming humor and personality - Dimension-switching mechanic is clever - Strong soundtrack - Exploration feels rewarding - Boss fights are memorable - Co-op mode is a lot of fun - One of the most stylish metroidvanias around  The bad: - Some platforming sections become brutally difficult - Combat can get repetitive near the end - Certain enemy combinations are annoying - Backtracking may frustrate some players - Humor and references won’t work for everyone - Difficulty spikes happen occasionally - Story is fairly simple - Some challenge rooms can feel frustrating  Overall, Guacamelee! Super Turbo Championship Edition is a fantastic metroidvania with excellent combat, great humor, and tons of personality. It’s colorful, creative, and consistently entertaining from start to finish.

[h3]Resumo[/h3] Guacamelee é um jogo charmoso, com um humor bobão, mas de diversão garantida, o progresso é muito bem definido, e com um combate legal, mas que poderia ser melhor, se tirasse um pouco sua complexidade. As lutas contra chefes se destacam,pena que ao decorrer do jogo são poucas, deixando um gostinho de quero mais. [h3]Prós[/h3] [list] [*]História legal [*]Batalha de chefe [*]Arte [/list] [h3]Contras[/h3] [list] [*]Muitas mecânica de combate [/list] [h3]História[/h3] Gostei da história, apesar do grande clichê do herói que precisa salvar a amada das garras do vilão. Ainda que o clichê seja batido, a maneira com que o jogo conta essa história, misturando temas da cultura mexicana, principalmente a festa de Los muertos é interessante, [h3]Gameplay[/h3] O gameplay do jogo é bem fluido, e o game design em minha opinião funciona muito bem junto com a narrativa. A todo momento o jogador é recompensado por novas habilidades ou novas áreas que fazem o senso de progresso ser extremamente presente no jogo. [h3]Combate[/h3] Em linhas gerais o combate me agradou, mas de alguma maneira acho que eu esperava mais, ou menos. O jogo possui muitas mecânica e inúmeros diferentes combos, ainda assim o meu principal mata que foram as habilidades do bode. Acho que faltou um pouco.maisnde atenção para o jogador ficar mais curioso em relação aos especiais. As lutas contra chefes foram boas, principalmente por sair um pouco do padrão que vem vinha sendo, e por ter mecânicas únicas de cada chefe. Fiquei com um gostinho de quero mais lutas assim no jogo. [h3]Arte[/h3] Gostei da arte do jogo, o tema da festa dos mortos é algo muito difundido no mundo e com a cara do México. Confesso que não conheço muito a história que envolve o festival, mas a arte tem muita sinergia com o que conhecemos de México, além de ser incrivelmente bem desenhada.

Ótimo metroidvania com beat'em up, divertido, engraçado e cheio de referências.  Minha maior crítica a esse jogo é que a progressão de nível não é lá tão bem feita, sendo que sai de um ponto bastante fácil pra um que beira o impossível de forma abrupta.  De resto, acho que é um jogasso e um clássico indie

Un metroidvania ambientado en México que destaca muchísimo por su identidad. Su estética es muy colorida y llamativa, con un estilo artístico inspirado en la cultura mexicana que le da mucha personalidad.  Al inicio, los controles pueden sentirse un poco toscos, pero te acostumbras bastante rápido y terminan siendo satisfactorios, sobre todo cuando empiezas a encadenar habilidades. En cuanto a dificultad, es un metroidvania bastante accesible, ideal si no buscas algo demasiado exigente en combate.  Donde realmente brilla es en el plataformeo: tiene secciones que te hacen pensar y reaccionar bien, llegando a ser más desafiantes que los combates en sí.  En general, es una experiencia muy entretenida, con buen ritmo y perfecta si quieres iniciarte en el género metroidvania. Ofrece un sistema de combate y plataformeo accesible, pero con momentos de reto bien planteados que le dan un toque muy satisfactorio.

Aesthetically speaking, its pretty solid, but VERY dated at times, especially with all the "LOOK VIDEOGAME REFERENCE HAHAHA SO COOL VIDEOGAME I LOVE IT" easter eggs, but outside of that, its a fine looking game with a likeable artstyle  Gameplay wise, its a pretty sold beat em up, but it can get repetitive at times, even with the combat upgrades and movement options, I wouldn't say its bad, but it can get a bit boring.  Story wise, its eh. Nothing special, some dated references, but nothing offensive, and sometimes even a little funny.  Metrodvania wise, it's fine. Nothing too big, nothing too elaborate, and suprisingly easy to get into , even on hard more.   Overall, a solid 6/10, maybe a 7, but not a 5. Good for beginners, but if you want more challenging games in this genre, there are plenty others.

Guacamelee!  El indie que me devolvió la fe  Después de una seguidilla de decepciones, con un Hades 2, que sigo haciendo runs en mi cabeza y un Castlevania: Lords of Shadow que me hizo pensar que a veces es mejor no vivir, apareció Guacamelee! como un salvador en vida, devolviéndome las ganas de jugar. No es casualidad que al final el gaming lo salve un indie.  https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3708914435  Guacamelee! es un metroidvania bastante ligerito. De hecho, creo que sería el punto de entrada perfecto para alguien que quiere meterse al género, no exige demasiado y es cortito. La aventura de Juan está cargada de humor, de pollos y de referencias a la cultura de los videojuegos. Reconocerlas te hace sentir que le sabés a este hobby cada vez más caro (por favor, que la RAM baje).  Lo que más destaca son sus gráficos. Arte plano, con aire vectorial, pero no como los íconos insulsos de esta nueva era que nos dejó sin Frutiger Aero, sino algo mucho más lindo. Decoraciones, paisajes de pueblitos, selvas y templos que hacen que el juego se vea precioso. Unos capos los pibes de DrinkBox.  El diseño de personajes también está muy bien, tanto en representación como en carisma, ya que escuchando cada uno, se capta bien su personalidad y forma de ser. El Pollo que te enseña movimientos es un hallazgo, y los jefes son lo mejor.  https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3708945016  Viajar por los niveles se siente entretenido. Como buen metroidvania, desbloquear una habilidad te despierta curiosidad y te invita a explorar el mapa una y otra vez para ver qué más podés abrir. Todo se da de manera orgánica, sin sentirse repetitivo, ni cansador. Sin dudas, lo mejor del juego.  Eso sí,  Guacamelee! trata la cultura mexicana con los clichés más clichés que existen. No busca reinterpretar nada, y si lo hace no es tan radical. Pero… funciona. Y funciona muy bien.  En definitiva, una joya indie super recomendada. Un juego que demuestra que EA es una mierda, Ubisoft tiene que morir, Blizzard debería bajarle la suscripción al WoW y Valve tiene que sacar el Half-Life 3.  https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=3710882836  8.5/10

De los mejores metroidvania que he jugado, no es el mejor en nada, pero tampoco comete ningun error que muchos otros juegos cometen  Puntos positivos: -Puntos de guardado, probablemente lo mejor de este juego, hay un millon de puntos de guardado lo que se agradece porque siempre me ha frustrado morir en algun lugar alejado y tener que volver por donde acabas de pasar, esto no es un problema para este juego, incluso si eres un mal jugador por puro ensayo y error lograras pasar las partes que te cuesten -Personalizacion con la tienda, puedes comprar nuevos trajes que tienen mejoras y desventajas para adaptarse a tu modo de juego y comprar mejoras para algunos movimientos -Movilidad/combate a medida avanzas en el juego obtiene nuevas habilidades que no solo sirven para moverte mejor si no para combatir de mejor manera y obtener mejores combos -Beat them up, no soy mucho de este tipo de juegos, pero en este no fue un problema, porque los combates son bastante rapidos si sabes como combatir y si eres bueno con la esquiva no te golpean demasiado -Mapa, se agradece mucho que el mapa te marque los lugares donde hay algun potenciador y te de un porcentaje de cuanto te falta para sacar el 100% -Plataformeo si bien hay algunas zonas con plataformeo dificil, no es algo injusto y gracias al moviemiento no deberia ser un gran problema continuar -Indicador de a donde ir, durante todo el juego tienes una flecha en tu mapa que te indica donde debes ir para continuar la historia, esto a mi me sirvio bastante porque a veces no tenia mucho tiempo para jugar y eso me ayudaba a no olvidar a donde ir despues de unos dias sin jugar -Precio, este juego tiene ya un precio muy bajo pero ademas siempre sale con descuento  Puntos negativos: -Hay algunos enemigos molestos como el cactus, los enemigos sombras o los que se teletransportan, en especial cuando debes pelear con otros enemigos al mismo tiempo -Como mencione antes, hay algunas partes con plataformeo un poco dificil y esto podria ser frustrante si no se te da muy bien -Misiones, un problemita es que en este juego hay misiones secundarias, pero no se marcan en el mapa ni tampoco tienes una lista de ellas, asi que tienes que recordarlas tu mismo -Linealiadad, no hay mucha opcion para ir un camino secundario en este juego, la historia es lineal y si quieres puedes desviarte a obtener mejoras que no podias antes, pero nada mas -Duracion, no es un juego demasiado largo, tal vez 7-8 horas  Como dije, no esperes que este juego te cambie la vida, pero es un gran metroidvania que hace bien su trabajo y no comete errores, pero que vale mucho la pena

It was a very good metroidvania game. The combat never felt boring and all the mechanics iterated on itself. I found myself wondering how this game can get harder and as I played the enemy variety changed, and I had to start prioritizing certain enemies or else I couldn't clear a room.  I was very impressed at how everything came together at the end.

A great cool metroidvania with Mexican theme. I don't know any other metroidvania games which use Mexican culture as a basis. The game feels very fluid and fun. You can do great combos. It has a very fluid combat system. There are great areas to explore and secrets to find. There is a typical metroidvania style map to explore and some optional areas included too. All of this are combined with great Mexican soundtrack. The game makes me feel like I'm a master luchador whose on a quest to save his wife.  Overall, I think Guacamelee is a beautiful metroidvania.

Action metroidvania 101. This game is well-made, funny under every aspect, without excelling in any. A textbook example - especially given that it came out before many other similar games.  Combat is made of few moves, but it's responsive, varied enough not to be boring and with the perfect challenge; (puzzle-)platforming is more than enjoyable; story and characters are hilarious and the narration is very concise; navigation smooth and straight-forward (unlike many other games...).

Este es probablemente el juego de acción más enérgico y alegre que he jugado. El juego parece diseñado con buen humor y con una gran afición por los memes. El mundo de los vivos es vibrante, brillante y lleno de color, mientras que el mundo de los muertos es púrpura y místico. No hay momentos aburridos en el juego donde simplemente te mueves de un punto A a otro. Constantemente estás destrozando cosas, saltando y... luchando por saltar. Los enemigos se vuelven demasiado numerosos hacia el final, y si solo aprietas botones sin pensar, podrías terminar de mal humor. Pero estos pequeños inconvenientes quedan eclipsados ​​por la inmensa positividad.

Oyunun ana hikayesini bitirdim. Oyun kısa bir oyun. Eleştirilebilecek çok yanı var. Boss fightlar  çocuk  oyuncağı derecesinde kolay. Aralarda  olan mini zoraki fightlar da zor denemez ama bazıları sinir bozucu. Oyunda maskeler hiçbir işe yaramıyor. Hepsinin kullanmamanız geerektiren çok fazla negatif özelliği var. Parayla alınan özelliklerin de içi boş. Yaratıkları tutup fırlatmakta çoğu zaman işlevsiz neden oyunda var anlamadım. Parkur levelleri de aşırı kolay. Haritadaki hızlı seyahat noktaları da tamamen  gereksiz hiç kullanmaya gerek kalmıyor. Yani özetle oyuna eklenen hiçbir mekaniği kullanmıyorsunuz nerdeyse. Oyunun hikayesi sonu kadar klişe. Oyun bitince hiçbir şey hissetmiyorsunuz. Bu oyun ancak ya benim canım çok metroidvania türü oyun çekti ama diğer tüm oyunları yaladım yuttum, çıtır çerezlik ne var derseniz oynayabileceğiniz bir oyun. Bu oyunu ne zaman eklemişim kütüphaneme hatırlamıyorum ama büyük ihtimalle ücretsiz geçti elime. Asla para vermezdim de zaten.  Puanım: 2/5

This game has no right to feel this good to play. Every punch, slam and uppercut has that [i]chef’s kiss[/i] impact and the platforming puzzles are just tricky enough to make you feel smart without making you Google a walkthrough in shame. It’s colorful, funny and surprisingly demanding when it wants to be. Also, any game that lets me solve puzzles by turning into a chicken deserves respect. Guacamelee knows exactly what it is and commits hard and honestly, that confidence carries it. [b][8.5/10][/b]

A nice short-ish metroidvania with a solid artstyle, good movement/combat options and decent music.  Story is pretty basic, but the overall flow is actually not bad. Sometimes I felt like the areas were too large (mainly because of the arenas), but that isn't much of an issue. Combat is pretty fun! Doing some combos (especially involving throws) is very satisfying, if only higher combo number actually affected your combat options. Enemy variety is not bad and they flow well with combat, albeit some can be very annoying. Movement options like uppercuts are directly tied to your stamina, which means you WILL have to get more stamina with exploration - for combat I understand, otherwise not really. Some endgame challenges were a bit harsh (mainly the combo ones) and I wish the game had some way of letting you track remaining chests, as they can be very hidden.  I've had a good time with Guacamelee! Game is fun, not too long or short, looks good and has tons of references.

For some reason, I never posted a review of this game until now, but since I just reviewed the Gold Edition, I might as well.  This is one of my favorite games of all time, if not the number one. The gameplay is addictive as HELL.  I’m not a tryhard gamer or anything, I struggle with Dark Souls, barely made it through the first half of Hollow Knight, and Super Meat Boy still gives me trouble, but this game strikes such a perfect balance with its difficulty.  Hard mode enemies and optional platforming challenges required for 100% completion (looking at you, tree tops and the Super Mario Galaxy-ass disapearing platforms in Sierra Morena) made me ragequit more than once. But ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, finally beating them felt incredible. When I hit 100%, and got that nice ass ribbon, I reached a euphoric high usually reserved for descriptions of strong methamphetamines.  The story is great too. The dialogue is packed with sarcastic wit if that’s your thing, and the game is full of references if you’re paying attention. My favorite is the Purple Rain reference in Desertito Caliente in the World of the Dead.  Character designs are super charming, and I got way more invested in the character dynamics than I expected.  10/10 game. I’m literally a luchador trying to save my Presidente’s daughter from off-brand Jack Skellington <3

Nunca había tenido la oportunidad de probar si quiera este juego en su auge, pues no era fan de los metroidvania.. Pero los descuentos mágicos de Steam me trajeron hacía acá no hace mucho y realmente fue una experiencia muy agradable visitar sin expectativas ni comentarios externos este juego. Ya platinado, puedo decir con seguridad que es una carta de amor a los videojuegos, pues se nota las referencias hacía otras franquicias y el cariño que le metieron sus creadores para crear con un gameplay ya anteriormente visto, como lo es el género metroidvania, y así crear un mundo único y un gameplay dinámico que en ningún momento llega a sentirse tedioso. El sistema de combos además es muy gratificante y te incita realmente a alcanzar combos altos por lo sencillo que es de entender, más no siempre de dominar.  Eso si, un punto en contra que le doy es los desafios del Diablo en el infierno, no los sentí como una dificultad natural sino más bien como desafios artificiales para llenar una sección que no supieron aprovechar, pero al no ser una parte principal del juego sino más bien un agregado a este mismo, no le doy tanta importancia.  Realmente es agradable ver una representación latina.. En este caso, mexicana, en un juego con tanto carisma y tan bien pulido, muy recomendado si eres fan de los metroidvania o de encadenar combos con oleadas de enemigos.

This was another indie game I missed during the debut era in the early 2010's. While I wasn't keen on the beat-em up gameplay at first, I also heard this was part metroidvania and was willing to check it out. After many years, this is a game I was glad to finally play and master!  The biggest draw for me was the artstyle and presentation, being stylized heavily in Hispanic influence, especially with a Dia de los Muertos theme! Going along with what's offered, you play as a luchador who's gameplay style revolves around punches and pseudo-wrestling combos. It may sound a little complicated at first, but it's pretty straight forward that's easy to learn and rewarding to master the more moves you learn!  This "Super Turbo Championship Edition" is the definitive version of the game after the release of the original and Gold edition, updating and adding plenty to do and discover for yourself. For the most part, the game is accessible and enjoyable all the way through, but about halfway into the game, the puzzles like teleporting and combat can get very chaotic to keep up, even with all the tools at your disposal. There are no lives and there's plenty of checkpoints to alleviate this, but it can get frustrating at times if you aren't adapting to the challenge.  While the skill ceiling can be daunting for the average player, it's an insanely fun game to play that's easy to pick up, and with the added bonus of co-op, you can really wreck stuff up in this game! It's like a Metroid and River City Girls combined to form this; and as unconventional as that sounds, it's a battle that's worthy of the Metroidvania genre!

In guacamelee you play as Juan as he tries and saves the presidents daughter from the evil hands of Calaca, a evil man who comes from the depths of hell.   Guacamelee is a welcome take on the metroidvania style, with cartoonish looks but with soul for sure.  The biggest positive point for me is the actual characters and dialogue in the game, I found it very funny throughout the game and with a lot of character.  The movement in this game is smooth and the upgrades that you get throughout the game really improve your mobility and they make you feel better equiped to move throughout the map. These upgrades are also very well paced throughout the game,  I never felt that I was stuck with no upgrades for a long time.  Talking about upgrades its good to move onto the combat which I feel like its a way better combat system that a metroidvania game usually has, the amount of combos and possibilities that you can do is really incredible, and with every upgrade it builds upon the combos that you can already do, and it feels incredibly smooth to perform them.   In a metroidvania its always a problem the amount of backtracking that one has to do, although in my first playthorugh I did a lot of backtracking, with fear that I might be leaving something behind, when I did a second playthrough on hard mode, I realised that if I just go through the game and  with minimal side tracking, you almost don't need any backtracking at all, which is really nice, I think this is needed more, Also having from the get go a fast travel method is really good.  The boss fights in terms of personality and looks are all unique and they are fun, which is the point of the game, speaking on this though a more negative point is that I feel like the last bossfight is a bit underwhelming, although we have been building this fight up all game, when we do get there it feels easier than the boss fight just before it.  The story is nothing special, but I do find the ending touched me, I was not expecting the bad ending to be the way that it was, I was positively surprised by both endings.

The game that got me into metroidvanias. Fairly breezy experience with a fun combat system. Instant respawns give the more challenging platforming sections lower stakes while still remaining rewarding. A fun, lighthearted story with quirky characters and a great art style. In the past 5-10 years, more punishing games have gotten all the spotlight in this genre, but Guacamelee is still a blast.

너무 좋은 메트로베니아 게임들이 많이 나와서, 지금 플레이하기엔 아쉬운점이 많다. 일단 패드겜이라 키보드에서 조작감이 별로다. 키도 많아서 좀 헷갈린 것도 크다. 플랫폼같은건 크게 어렵지 않은데, 콤보쌓는 챌린지들이 너무 어렵다. 콤보를쌓으려면 한대도 안맞아야되는데, 점프 대쉬가 없어서 피하는게 좀 어렵다. 적중에 대쉬로 피하는 애들도 있고 공중몹잡는게 어렵다. 전투는 잡기, 던지기, 강펀치 등등 괜찮은데, 보스는 당연히 안 잡히고 그렇다보니 보스전을 쉽게 만들어놨다. 퀘스트, 의상 등등 추가적인 요소들이 있지만, 매력이 약하다...  보상이 돈, 체력조각, 스태미나조각, 임펄소조각, 유령코인중 하나라서 굳이 맵마다 100퍼센를 채워야하는 동기가 안생긴다. 플레이 타임도 짧다. 클리어에 4시간 반 걸렸고, 챌린지랑 오브캐고 진엔딩보는데 7시간 걸렸다. 맵100퍼작 하려다가 1~2개씩 안보여서 그냥 포기했고  메트로베니아로는 입문하기에 좋다고 생각하고 플랫적인건 초보~중간정도인거 같다.

Another day, another look at games I played in the last decade before a long hiatus.  Maybe not a memory as amazing as the first [b]Ori[/b] game, but I had a great time with [b]Guacamelee[/b], so great that I in fact ended up playing this [b]Super Turbo Championship Edition[/b] later, too. It even acknowledges being a later update ironically, as at the end of an important early cutscene, the additional boss pops up asking "missed something?"  The graphics are so stylized, colorful, and lively, and like any good metroidvania, every new ability opens up a lot of new areas and possibilities. I love how the uppercut attack works as a double jump before you find a true double jump (and for quite a while I thought there wouldn't be one).  I'm still not a big fan of the "arenas" where, from time to time, you are trapped to fight bunches of enemies, even more so as this edition forces you to also use the Intenso power - not like it's not fun, but I'd have liked for combat encounters to flow a bit better.  It's really not much compared to how fun the game was as a whole. Maybe it's a case of putting the sequel among the next games to finally play, while waiting for [b]Blighted[/b]...

墨西哥英雄大混戰是一款用墨西哥亡靈節與摔角包裝而成的類銀河戰士惡魔城遊戲。劇情是標準的英雄救美，角色刻畫上多著墨於反派，主角反而因為無口又缺乏足夠個性的動作，使其始終是個極為無聊的角色。  戰鬥以輕、重擊+翻滾為基礎，重擊消耗能量，旅途中能夠學習到搭配各種方向所使出的重擊招式。但他卻為此配上了不同顏色的屬性，敵人套上了屬性護盾，沒用對攻擊就沒傷害。遊戲界最糞設計之一，還喜歡堆怪，使整個戰鬥體驗很狗屎。同時敵人設計很喜歡卡在操作極限，常常連招接不上，剛翻滾完或揮完一拳又被打飛，摔角動作是個陷阱，常使自身處於不利，讓整個戰鬥不是很好玩。關卡設計表裡關視覺變化初看驚奇，後面開始要不斷切換來闖關玩起來很痛苦，還出現躲在裡關的敵人，你打不到他他打得到你，配上堆怪糞不可言。  整體而言，除了亡靈節風格外可取外，是個讓我玩起來無聊又痛苦的遊戲。

Замечательная метроидвания, которая идеально подходит для новичков (вроде меня). В игре элементарная и очень приятная боевая система с различными комбо-атаками и специальными ударами, а также четко выверенный платформинг с постоянно растущими ставками, где разработчики не заставляют часами бегать от контрольной точки, чтобы заново пропрыгать один и тот же сегмент, а создают максимально сбалансированный опыт, дабы ты и головой подумал, но не сгорел. Играть всегда было интересно и весело, ситуации сменяются одна за другой, и увлекательно исследовать уровни на поиски секретных сундуков с золотом для прокачки или находить части улучшения для здоровья/маны.  Слегка подпортили впечатления финальные этапы, в особенности порталы, которые взялись из ниоткуда и заставили к себе долго привыкнуть, что слегка порушило динамику, ведь до финала было рукой подать. Но при этом игра всё равно не напрягает на нормальном уровне сложности, от чего в финале, даже в битве с главным боссом, эмоции переполнены восторгом.  Я бы поставил ей 8/10. Местами было к чему придраться, но поиграть стоит абсолютно каждому, особенно если до этого метроидвании вас не привлекали. Здесь минимум раздражающих моментов, а также замечательный визуальный стиль, мексиканский колорит раскрыт на 100%, есть какой-никакой сюжет и клевые персонажи, а также немало смешных или просто потешных моментов. Настоятельно рекомендую.

Guacamelee! is a game that I resisted playing for a long time, mostly because of my personal trauma with Metroidvanias. I have played in my life many games with Metroidvania aspects, such as Alien: Isolation and even Dark Souls. But few were the times I played those purebred 2D Metroidvania games - and those games really pushed my buttons. For you kids who weren't around back in the day, Metroidvanias were games that demanded a lot of memory and backtracking from players, making them aggravating for those of us who saw no fun in that.  But times changed, and much in the same way games perfected themselves to be more gamer-friendly, Guacamelee! shows that Metroidvanias too can catch up with more modern trends. And as such, it is the first true Metroidvania I truly loved. Of course there are still some aspects of it that brought me some PTSD - namely the treasure hunting. But for most part, it's an incredibly fun game, full of love from its developers.  For starters, as the title implies, this version of the game is expanded, adding a few things here and there. I never played the original version, but I can see playing the game as Tostada - originally a female NPC who appears at some cutscenes - is one of such additions. Now, when I describe this game as a Metroidvania, I pretty much describe about half of it: you gain powers as you progress, thus giving you access to areas that were previously inaccessible. But the game does a tremendous job in creating an unique identity for itself, which made it distinctive and celebrated in the Metroidvania appreciating community.   The world can get quite big, but there's always a quest marker pointing you where to go. You're never lost on which direction to take, in that the game guides you to your next mission. The thing however - and this is where the game gets a bit clunky - is that to collect the previously locked treasures of an area, you have to backtrack and cover large distances to come and go. Thankfully, blocked paths appear on the map as squares with colours matching the move they need to be dis-obstructed. This lets you know where the places you couldn't access before are, thus facilitating things a lot. Plus, the game does have a fast travel system in form of these giant stone heads, and they are of enormous help to move around the world, yes. But still, you have to go to these stones to access the net of shortcuts, and it would have been much better - even for a matter of locomotion - if players could teleport to these heads from wherever they are. To aggravate things more, some locations don't have these heads. This is a massive blow against the game, since it affects so directly its treasure hunting aspect, making it not fun.  The combat however is the best aspect of the game. Being based in combos and dodging, it was actually fun to dispose of multiple enemies in an almost Arkham style. And the best part is that the powers you gain along the way become a direct part of combat: they're not there just to just open paths, and I saw myself incorporating them to combat. On top of that, you eventually get the Intenso, which is this power mode that makes you much more stronger, and charges with combat. Many times, you'll be locked in a room until you dispose of all enemies, and when you're done, a massive piñata will appear with a prize inside, letting you that bout ended.  Some enemies are your regular minions, while others move more quicker and dodge your attacks. And then there are the cursed ranged ones, tossing projectiles at you in the middle of a fight, or even during platforming, and they suck! As you progress, you'll find enemies who are more complicated, requiring different techniques: some enemies have shields with different colours, meaning that to break them, you have to hit them with the right move - but thankfully, Intenso can break any shield of any colour. Some enemies have multiple shields, and some enemies are shown as silhouettes, signalling that they're at another plan of reality, and you have to switch to fight them back.  Speaking of which, this is one of the most famous aspects of the game - the ability to traverse between the worlds of the living and the dead. Different worlds have their differences, such as platforms and barriers that appear and disappear. Many puzzles and fights demand quick inputs. Another power is to become a chicken, granting you access to smaller passages. This game is very much a platformer, and some platforming can be very tricky, specially at later levels. And remember that your powers are limited by a stamina-like bar, and that you can't perform the same fighting move more than once at a jump.  As you explore the world, you collect golden coins that you use to buy upgrades at checkpoints. Unfortunately, this is one of those games in which, once you buy everything, the gold starts pilling up. Then there are the purple coins, that you use to buy new costumes at the checkpoints. And these costumes have buffs and debuffs: they may grant you an advantage along with a disadvantage - you know how it is. Naturally, you'll also find upgrades for your health, stamina and Intenso capacities - they lie in chests, but you may also buy some of them. And there are even some side quests to be done, but don't expect quest marks for these ones.  Now, there are some elusive collectables that I recommend you to catch, which are the orbs. These things are incredibly well hidden - as in, you'll need walkthroughs for that crap. And once you find the hidden entrance for each of the orbs, you usually have to traverse a very demanding platforming section that will have you cursing at the devs. These orbs are hard to get, but once you do, you'll gain a fantastic power: the ability to fly freely as the chicken. This pretty much trivialises most of the other moves, and it's great for exploring. In fact, I suggest you to only go treasure hunting when you get that power. That's my advice: if you want to 100% this game, get the orbs as soon as you're able. Also, the orbs are important to get the real ending.  There's also the Pollo Bomb, which clears purple blocks, and it's not mandatory to get to the main boss. I kept on playing the game, believing that at some point I would be introduced to it like it had been happening with the other moves. But you get it in the Hell level, as a reward for completing a series of challenges. That wasn't very clear to me, but maybe it was my fault for not paying attention.  Last but certainly not least, the presentation. Guacamelee! is a gorgeous game, with a marvellous soundtrack to match (which also changes with the realities). The humorous dialogues, the character designs, the scenarios, everything is very pleasing to the eyes and ears. I find incredible that it took a group of Canadian developers to make one of the most wonderful love letters to Mexican culture that I know. But unfortunately... ugh, the pop culture references! The game is just littered with them! I admit that spotting the Me Gusta meme really brought me back to 2010. But most of it is just annoying as hell, and they dent what is otherwise a beautiful game. I mean, one of the moves is called Das Boot. Because when I'm playing a Metroidvania brawler themed after Mexican culture and folklore, I want to be reminded of 1980's German marine war movies.  In the end, Guacamelee! is not perfect in some aspects, but its highs go very high, thus deserving its classic status among Metroidvanias.

I enjoy Guacamelee! overall. Its primary focus is on beat-’em-up combat and platforming, and it blends those two elements well. The combat is satisfying, especially with how you can stagger enemies and throw them into each other to control crowds. It’s fast, punchy, and feels great once everything clicks.  [h2]Gameplay & Platforming[/h2] The inputs can feel a little stiff, which means you need to be very precise—especially during platforming sections. For simply completing the main story, this isn’t a big problem, and most of the platforming is fairly smooth. But if you’re going for secrets or optional challenges, some of those areas can be surprisingly demanding and occasionally frustrating.  [h2]Story & Pacing[/h2] I’m not a fan of the story interruptions. They’re mostly front-loaded, but they still break the pacing early on. Once the game settles into its rhythm, though, the focus shifts back to what it does best: combat and platforming.  [h2]Exploration[/h2] I appreciate that the game clearly marks objectives and generally keeps you moving forward. I like this direction-focused approach, though others may find it makes the game feel more like an action-platformer than a true Metroidvania. If you enjoy wandering and getting lost in a big interconnected world, Guacamelee! might feel a little too guided. But if you just want solid combat, tight movement challenges, and clear progression, it’s a great fit.  [h2]Conclusion[/h2] Overall, Guacamelee! is one of the better Metroidvanias. It has fun combat, memorable movement challenges, and a strong sense of style. Some rigid inputs and pacing hiccups hold it back a bit, but as a whole, it’s an excellent game.

Unfortunately, the game is dull. I say “unfortunately” only for those who might rate it positively; as for me, I approached it skeptically from the start, expected nothing, and therefore wasn’t disappointed — I simply came to the conclusion that if a game becomes uninteresting to be in literally within the first 10 minutes, right after the protagonist (spoiler).  I played through about 50% of the game, just exploring it, and throughout that entire time I didn’t feel any enjoyment — not from the gameplay, not from the locations, not from the monsters, not from the bosses. There are a few small, insignificant positive moments — the fire guy antagonist, the luchador turning into a chicken… and that’s it.  The characters are dry, just characters and nothing more. Only the “fire guy” lights up the screen whenever he appears.  The visuals are simply bleak. I kept staring at the monitor, blankly looking at it and seeing nothing except the nice skin of the female character wearing a Día de los Muertos–style mask.  At the 50% mark I got stuck and couldn’t understand what I had missed. I don’t want to go back to look for the wall-climbing ability, or to farm currency for upgrades in boring combat, in boring locations. Especially with the extremely irritating trials in El Inferno — hell in the most literal sense.  Overall, this product left an even worse impression on me than Rusted Moss.  Thanks for your attention.  One more thing.  “Guacamelee Super Turbo Champion Edition offers one of the best-designed and most original takes on the well-worn metroidvania phenomenon you'll ever find.” 4.5 / 5 – US Gamer  This quote is displayed on the product page. Best-designed, well-worn. I sincerely hope no one takes this seriously today. Back in 2014 it was probably something fresh and unique, but now it simply doesn’t stand for anything.  I usually try to examine games more closely in my reviews and highlight any distinctive features if I can find them, but here I genuinely don’t know what else to add. You can, just like I did, play it yourself and take a look — maybe it will click for you, or maybe you’ll quit even earlier than I did.

NOTA: 6 de 10  Acción en plataformas 2D con ambientación mexicana que puede jugarse en cooperativo o individual. Las mecánicas permiten saltar, realizar ataques simples, combos y golpes sobrenaturales. Los mapas son al estilo Metroidvania, forzando a pasar varias veces por el mismo sitio tras patearse largas distancias.  Audio, música y gráficos de aceptable calidad mediante dibujos animados, con un estilo levemente repulsivo.  Físicas y animaciones mediocres, sobre todo en las peleas.  Historia simplista y forzada, que no se toma nada en serio, con personajes insoportablemente infantiles, y sin ninguna gracia. Cinemáticas que transcurren en el propio motor del juego, pero no permite saltarlas.  Opciones de configuración deficientes, solo deja redefinir controles en el teclado, con el mando no. De hecho, el mío de PlayStation no lo reconoce, salvo que se utilice alguna aplicación externa como [u][url=https://github.com/AntiMicroX/antimicrox]Antimicrox[/url][/u]  Optimización capada a 60 FPS, a pesar de asignar la frecuencia del monitor a 144 y desactivar la sincronización vertical.  Idioma traducido al español en los textos, voces no hay.  Dificultad supuestamente separada en al menos 2 niveles, pero no he visto forma de cambiarla. Quizás te obliga a terminar el juego en el nivel "normal" antes de poder jugarlo en "difícil". IA rudimentaria y lenta, que se atasca cuando el protagonista se encuentra por encima del nivel de los enemigos, por ejemplo en unas escaleras.  Tutoriales internos sí tiene, indicando que teclas presionar en ciertos momentos. El problema es que la interfaz a veces muestra teclas que no se corresponden con las que se han redefinido.  Guardar-cargar partida se efectúa solo en puntos de control, que pueden estar más o menos separados entre sí.   Conclusión: no es un mal juego, pero sí bastante mediocre y repetitivo. Me esperaba más teniendo en cuenta que goza de un 92 % de reseñas positivas.

Guacamelee! Super Turbo Championship Edition is an absolute masterpiece of a game, an electrifying metroidvania experience that is a joy to play from start to finish. It's rare for a game to nail so many elements so perfectly, but this title does exactly that.  The combat system is the beating heart of the game, delivering a fantastic mix of fast-paced brawling, lucha libre throws, and rewarding complexity. Stringing together combos feels incredibly satisfying, and the moment-to-moment action is constantly engaging.  Equally impressive is the platforming. The game introduces a "dimension-swapping" mechanic that is pure genius, providing innovative puzzles and challenging sequences that are a genuine thrill to figure out and execute. The level design is top-tier, encouraging exploration and making use of your expanding abilities in clever ways.  Visually, the game is stunning. The art style is vibrant, colorful, and teeming with personality, drawing heavily from Mexican culture and folklore in the best ways possible. The excellent soundtrack perfectly complements the visuals, creating a lively and immersive atmosphere.  With charming characters, a great sense of humor, and plenty of secrets to uncover, Guacamelee! STCE is a must-play title. It's a joyful, challenging, and incredibly polished game that deserves a spot in any gamer's library. ¡Viva Guacamelee!

Finished the main story on my Steam Deck OLED. What a fantastic game and a perfect fit for handheld play!  Guacamelee! STCE is a vibrant, hilarious, and incredibly tight Metroidvania. The brawler-style combat feels chunky and satisfying, and the genius part is how your combat moves slowly become your platforming tools, constantly opening up the world. The Mexican folklore-inspired aesthetic is a joy to behold and really pops on the Deck's OLED screen.  Performance was flawless. I ran the game installed on a Micro SD card on Linux SteamOS and experienced zero issues—it's clearly very well-optimized. The drop-in co-op is a great bonus if you have a friend around.  I didn't 100% it (those last few chests are tricky!), but I enjoyed every moment of Juan's ridiculous adventure. If you love Metroidvanias and have a Steam Deck, this is an easy recommendation.

Guacamelee! Super Turbo Championship Edition, developed by Drinkbox Studios and published alongside Kepler Ghost, is a vibrant and spirited love letter to both Mexican folklore and the classic Metroidvania genre. Set in a universe where the realms of the living and the dead overlap, the game tells the story of Juan Aguacate, an unassuming agave farmer whose life takes a surreal turn when his friend and love interest, Lupita, is kidnapped by the villainous skeleton warlord Carlos Calaca. Granted the powers of a magical luchador mask, Juan embarks on a wild adventure that takes him through colorful villages, haunted temples, and mystical dimensions, all while punching, kicking, and body-slamming an army of undead enemies. It’s a journey filled with humor, energy, and an infectious sense of joy that captures the heart of both Mexican culture and fast-paced action design.  From the very start, Guacamelee! establishes its personality with an explosion of color and rhythm. Every environment bursts with life—sun-soaked towns adorned with papel picado banners, dusty graveyards bathed in eerie blues, and fiery underworld catacombs alive with flickering light. The art style feels hand-painted, with bold outlines and saturated colors that create the impression of a living cartoon. The soundtrack, a lively mix of traditional mariachi influences and pulsing electronic beats, ties it all together, ensuring that every punch and jump feels like part of a musical performance. What’s remarkable is how well the visual and auditory elements work together to sustain the game’s upbeat tone; there’s a sense of celebration even in the midst of chaos, and the world feels both fantastical and deeply rooted in cultural flavor.  The gameplay is where Guacamelee! truly shines. It blends high-energy brawling mechanics with tight platforming and exploration in a way that feels seamless. Combat is dynamic and layered—Juan can chain together punches, throws, and special moves into fluid combos that feel both responsive and powerful. Over time, you unlock new wrestling-inspired abilities, each doubling as a platforming tool. A flying uppercut lets you reach higher platforms, a frog slam breaks barriers beneath your feet, and a powerful dash punch lets you burst through obstacles and enemies alike. These abilities are color-coded to correspond with similarly marked walls and enemies, turning every battle and environmental puzzle into a satisfying dance of rhythm and reaction. The dual-purpose design of every skill makes exploration feel rewarding; each new upgrade doesn’t just enhance combat—it reshapes how you navigate the world.  Equally inventive is the game’s use of dimensional shifting. At various points, Juan must swap between the worlds of the living and the dead, with each version of reality offering unique layouts, hazards, and enemy placements. This mechanic adds a layer of strategic thinking, as you must time your jumps and attacks while transitioning between dimensions mid-combat or during intricate platforming sections. These sequences can be challenging, but they also represent some of the most creative design in the game, testing reflexes and observation in equal measure. The addition of local co-op enhances this experience even further, turning difficult challenges into collaborative moments of chaotic fun, as two players leap, dodge, and suplex enemies in perfect sync.  The Super Turbo Championship Edition improves upon the original release by refining pacing and adding new content, including additional levels, bosses, and expanded combat options. It also introduces new enemies and upgrades, tightening the overall balance and making the progression feel smoother. The enhanced visuals and performance upgrades make an already polished game feel even more fluid, especially during the fast-paced boss battles that push players to master all of Juan’s abilities. These bosses, often hilarious in design, serve as set pieces that mix humor with intensity, ensuring that the action never loses its playful tone.  Beneath its humor and chaos, Guacamelee! carries subtle themes of identity, sacrifice, and destiny. Juan’s transformation from humble farmer to heroic luchador mirrors the mythic structure of many folk tales—a story of ordinary people who rise to greatness through courage and love. Yet the game never takes itself too seriously. The writing is filled with tongue-in-cheek jokes, pop culture references, and self-aware nods to classic gaming tropes. It’s a world where you can fight skeletons one moment and stumble across a poster referencing “Mega Hombre” or “El Mariachi Bros” the next. This blend of reverence and parody keeps the game’s tone light and engaging, allowing players to enjoy its story without ever feeling weighed down by it.  If there’s one criticism to be made, it’s that the game’s structure can sometimes feel segmented. The interconnected world, while expansive, is divided by loading zones that slightly interrupt exploration. The difficulty curve also fluctuates; some of the platforming sections and late-game battles can be punishingly precise, especially for newcomers to the genre. Yet these are minor blemishes on an otherwise well-balanced experience. Once you grasp the rhythm of its combat and traversal, the game becomes an exhilarating test of skill and timing, rewarding mastery with a satisfying sense of flow.  Guacamelee! Super Turbo Championship Edition stands as a triumph of style, humor, and mechanical design. It’s a game that knows exactly what it wants to be—a celebration of lucha libre heroics, cultural folklore, and classic Metroidvania adventure. Its blend of fast-paced combat, clever exploration, and comedic storytelling makes it both approachable and deeply enjoyable. Few games manage to feel so exuberant while maintaining tight mechanical polish, and even fewer fuse art and gameplay as seamlessly as this one does. Whether you’re drawn in by its humor, its heart, or simply the thrill of suplexing skeletons in a world bursting with color, Guacamelee! is a joyous experience that stands tall among the greats of its genre.  Rating: 9/10

Una buena iniciación a los metroid-vanias.  Habiéndolo jugado en 2025, tras otros juegos del género como Hollow Knight y Blasphemous, me parece un poco difícil e injusto juzgar este. He de decir también que lo he jugado directamente en modo Difícil (desbloqueado con el truco de "arriba, arriba, abajo, abajo, izquierda, derecha izquierda, derecha, A, W, Espacio"), para que lo tengamos como referencia.  Está muy bien si quieres un juego de este género, pero no quieres echar tantas horas y no quieres una dificultad tan endiablada (en modo normal, porque en difícil hay algunas partes que dan ganas de estampar el mando). Por el precio no puedo decir nada, ya que te lo encuentras en muchas partes a euro y pico, que es un café. La originalidad e innovación hay que juzgarla teniendo en cuenta que es un juego de 2013, y se ve en la variedad de enemigos, que el camino es mucho más directo, la historia bastante poco desarrollada...   Sin embargo, sí que tiene cosas muy originales en algunas mecánicas que vas desbloqueando más tarde.  Le doy el pulgar arriba por el año en que salió, el precio al que te lo puedes encontrar, que puede estar dirigido a cierto público y porque es una experiencia más contenida y, aún así, muy currada. Lo que no puedo hacer es recomendarlo por encima de los ya mencionados Hollow Knight y Blasphemous en cuanto lo que es un producto redondo se refiere.

[h3]Плюсы ❤️[/h3] - Симпатичный визуальный стиль - Много отсылок на другие игры - Использование способностей не только в сражениях, но и в платформинге - Локальный кооп - Оптимальная продолжительность - Почти всю игру понятно, куда идти и что делать  [h3]Минусы 💔[/h3] - Иногда музыка играет не в тему - В последней трети игры резко повышается сложность - Обычная концовка не приносит ощущения завершенности произведения (а “правильная” концовка - это слишком задротская) - Секции для платформинга не очень хорошо играются в коопе (зачастую, один их проходит, а второй ожидает)  [h1]Оценка: 3/5[/h1]

Sono un pò perplesso nei riguardi di questo gioco. Se da un lato apprezzo molto lo stile artistico ed il combattimento, dall'altro non posso fare altro che notare delle evidenti storture e sbilanciamenti che inevitabilmente abbassano il livello complessivo di questo metroidvania. Innanzi tutto, come detto, lo stile del gioco è secondo me non solo molto apprezzabile, ma originalissimo. Non sono molti i videogiochi così tanto ispirati alla cultura ed alle credenze della cultura messicana e senza alcun dubbio lo stile del gioco è uno dei suoi punti forti e di distinzione. Il combattimento poi, credo che sia molto ben strutturato. Di base si hanno attacchi normali, salti e rotolate a cui presto saranno affiancati altre possibilità, come schiacciate, uppercut, dash e testate. Inoltre il gioco permette di afferrare e lanciare certi nemici dopo che avranno subito una certa soglia di danno, che se lanciati in direzione di altri nemici potranno danneggiare anche quest'ultimi. Combinare le varie mosse, quindi, da molta soddisfazione durante i combattimenti. Degne di segnalazione anche tutte le boss-fight, che sono sufficientemente varie e ognuna con le proprie meccaniche peculiari. Per quel che riguarda invece le possibilità di movimento ed esplorazione, qui si segue il mantra dei metroidvania. Imparerete infatti il doppio salto, la possibilità di rimpicciolirvi, la possibilità di scalare i muri, di proiettarvi da un muro verso l'altro estremo della stanza e di cambiare mondo di gioco passando alla dimensione dei morti. Tutto quello che ci si aspetta da un metroidvania... forse anche troppo, nel senso che è esattamente quello che ci si aspetta da un metroidvania e saranno soluzioni che in altri titoli simili avrete sperimentato innumerevoli volte. A dire il vero, il fatto che i nuovi poteri sblocchino altre aree è vero fino ad un certo punto, nel senso che è estremamente improbabile che decidiate ad un certo punto di tornare indietro in aree passate per sbloccare quella via che prima era inaccessibile... anche perchè sarà il gioco stesso a portarvi e guidarvi dove dovrete andare. Ebbene si, il gioco in realtà è abbastanza lineare ed a meno che non siate completisti o vogliate arrivare super preparati al finale, vi basterà seguire il percorso standard che il gioco propone. Devo dire inoltre che non ho apprezzato per niente il platforming e tutte quelle meccaniche che ci permettono di superare gli ostacoli forniti dai livelli. Innanzi tutto il salto è estremamente impreciso e capiterà spesso che si possa fare errori di calcolo per i salti, inoltre l'idea che alcune posizioni per essere raggiunte dovranno essere abbinate all'upercut è per me decisamente pessima. Non solo, ci sono sezioni di gioco dove vi sarà richiesto di eseguire diverse azioni in sequenza, tra salti, doppi salti, dash, uppercut, cambio di mondo e rotolata, roba che vi manderà facilmente al manicomio. E questo è evidente già circa a metà gioco, dove inspiegabilmente si ha una impennata improvvisa della difficoltà richiesta per poter muoversi all'interno del gioco. E' quindi palese che il gioco soffra di mal bilanciamento dalla metà in poi, fino al finale dove quei sadici degli sviluppatori han pensato bene di realizzare sezioni di gioco inutilmente contorte o che richiedono di imparare a memoria la sequenza di tasti da utilizzare se si vuole avere successo. Ebbene si, un gioco che inizialmente si era dimostrato piacevolissimo da giocare si è trasformato nel classico gioco try and error, dove la difficoltà diventa palesemente artificiale.  La struttura dell'intero gioco è alquanto discutibile e per certi versi ridondante. A sequenze esplorative si alternano sempre degli stanzoni che vengono chiusi dove spawneranno i nemici e quindi tutto questo si continuerà a ripetere fino al finale.  E' stato inoltre strano il fatto che nelle fasi finali gli sviluppatori hanno pensato bene di imbottire le stanze di nemici e di realizzare dei nemici alquanto fastidiosi da combattere, tra cui alcuni dove dovrete, oltre a cambiare mondo per poterli colpire, anche colpirli appositamente con un potere apposito per poter rompere il loro scudo che li protegge dagli attacchi, nel mentre che gli altri nemici, presenti nell'altro mondo quindi non colpibili, continueranno a rompervi le scatole. Guacamelee è quindi un gioco dalle due facce, divertentissimo in certi momenti, frustante in altri. Il risultato è un metroidvania nella media, che possiamo nel complesso considerare un buon gioco, nonostante tutto, ma che è ben lontano dai mostri sacri del genere come Hollow knight, che nonostante sia un gioco probabilmente anche più difficile di questo, è immensamente più piacevole da giocare.  VOTO: 7

A Quick One Before the Eternal Worm Devours Toronto (Thank God!!!)  If there was ever a game that was part of that era of indie game that walked the well-treaded path that Braid braved first, but was still weighed down by the """humor""" of the early 2010s, it's Guacamelee. One of the first games to bring back and even being to solidify the sub-genre of "Metroidvania", Drinkbox Studios knew that they had to hit this out the park when they rolled this out. This being one of their first major releases (no one remembers Tales From Space), it was all or nothing, it would either make a splash and make ripples that would influence or fall flat on its face. Can we look back at this game and see confidently that this succeeded and accomplished all that it set out to do?  No, not at all, it's why I'm reviewing Super Turbo Championship Edition and not Gold/the Original.  It took Drinkbox Studio two times to make this game okay and most of that is due to the looks of the game and the sound of the music. The soundtrack is actually really good the more I think about it, it really has that driving force that pushes you along the game, always high energy, but never grating or tiring. The way this game looks is another mark for it, it's really lively, it's vibrant, no expenses were spared in the making of the stages, the characters, and the backgrounds. It's great.  I wish I can say the same about the gameplay. It's not horrible by any means, but I wouldn't call it particularly good either. It's all sorta limp and nebulous. When you can play on the hard mode of a game and make it out of the other side of the tunnel by basically doing the same combo over and over with maybe a headbutt/uppercut here or there to get past some of the move-specific shields, we got a problem. I did not change it up much during combat, no switch-up, no change, just straight oatmeal and I beat the game with little effort. Hell, most of my deaths was due to the platforming and lava pits which weren't even that big of a problem because this game gives you checkpoint after checkpoint. If the punishment for dying/failing is backtracking like 2-3 rooms, where is the excitement gonna come from? Why would I bother trying if the most Imma get is a slap on the wrist?  Another thing that bothered me about this game is the journey to 100%. If there is a genre that wants the player to get every thing, it's the metroidvania. A good metroidvania leaves bread crumbs for you, it litters the maps, the halls, rooms, walls, courtyards, and everything in between with these little goodies, the player wants them, but they can't get them. Yet. Progression is the name of the game here and nothing is better than getting an upgrade or item that makes your character better while also progressing the game forward. A double jump is useful. An uppercut is useful, especially when some enemies require an uppercut now. Same goes for a wallrun, a headbutt, a smash, a dash, a kick, the ability to shrink down, the ability to switch between realms on the fly. All these things are not only useful for traversal, the combat much more open, but the game begins to require these power-ups. You gotta use everything you got to win, to be the hero. To finally get that 100 ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ percent.  You wanna know what isn't useful or fun? Having to get gold medals in challenges in order to get an absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ egg bomb that has no other purpose other than exploding marked blocks. That's it. It doesn't make you better, it doesn't get you more movement, you can barely use it during combat and even when you do, it sucks. Why bother designing it like this? It's like the Balan Wonderworld costumes that do only ONE thing poorly for one section of the game. It's straight up ass.  Remember that humor I mentioned early? You wanna date your game? Put in a meme. Like, bro, you're telling me that an entire dev team looked at Business Cat and thought it was evergreen humor? For ♥♥♥♥'s sake, one of your moves is called the Dashing Derpderp. C'mon man, even back in 2014, we were better than this.  Guacamelee is a pretty package, gently tied in a bow, wrapped with care. It's a shame that the only things inside it are 99 cent wrestler toys that smell weird, tamarindo candy wrappers and Michaels Dia de los Muertos made in china plastic skulls.   Two two's my word croski, you don't like Guacamelee, you're dess nons, you're straight gerbert fam, wasteyute. You're just miskeens, i catch you, it's wrapski styll. I know your cyattie's batttried by the mandem, crodie.  That's Toronto slang. You read what I typed and you tell me that Toronto shouldn't be consumed by the Coming Fire and burned to ash.

Guacamelee - цікава метроїдванія відносно візуального стилю, яка натхненна іспанською культурою.  [u]Сюжет[/u], на мою думку, доволі простий, але в цей же час незвичний, адже, як я і сказав на початку, весь ЛОР гри побудований на основі елементів іспанської культури. Особисто мене сюжет не захопив, але знайомство з культурою іншої країни є корисним досвідом. Також в грі присутній гумор, що теж є плюсиком. [u]Геймплей[/u] по суті як і у всіх метроїдваній: отримуй нові абілки під час проходження гри та повертайся в попередні локації, що дібратися туди, куди раніше не було доступу. Зі всіх механік я міг би виділити лише [spoiler]переміщення між світами[/spoiler], не знаю жодну метроїдванію, яка дозволяє на постійній основі користуватися цим моментом. Щодо ворогів - з однієї сторони кількість видів звичайних ворогів доволі велика (більше 10 точно), а з іншої в них відносно схожі атаки, якщо не враховувати різницю між абсолютно різними типами мобів. Більшою мірою вони всі проходяться не складно, але коли їх багато, то приходиться несолодко. Тепер боси, на дивно вони дуже прості, як мінімум всі окрім останнього і, можливо, передостаннього. Не тільки в плані складності, а й різномаїття їх атак. Я очікував більшого від них.  Як я вже казав, гра має доволі цікавий і при цьому простий [u]візуальний стиль[/u]. Думаю частково це теж завдяки іспанській культурі. Багів не помітив, гра я думаю потягне на будь-якому комп'ютері, який зможе запустити хоча б віндовс 7.  [u]Музичне супроводження[/u] все в тій же стилістиці. Занурює в цю іспанську атмосферу, але при цьому її різномаїття невелике, постійно помічав, що грає приблизно одна й та ж мелодія, хоча вона не встигла набриднути.  [u]Підсумовуючи[/u], гра, на мій суб'єктивний розсуд, доволі посередня, але дарує цікавий експіріенс в плані ознайомлення з культурою згаданої вище країни. Якщо любите метроїдванні, любите Іспанію та є зайві гривні, то можна за знижкою спробувати купити та пограти. Але якщо пропустите гру - багато ви не втратите

Guacamelee! Super Turbo Championship Edition - это больше чем простой платформер! Игра вышла крутая, много локаций, разные суперсилы, которые ты можешь чередовать чтобы убить как можно быстрее врага и многое другое. Графика в игре приятная, красивые локации, которые передают всю атмосферу игры. Сюжет не блистает, но концовка сделана красиво! Игра вполне на 5-6 часов и моментами игра может быть сложной даже на normal сложности, но если выучить способности врага и как он работает то, будет уже не так и трудно. Вообщем я ставлю этой игре 7/10 :)

A good metroidvania. I didn't play the Gold edition, so this is my first time play Super Turbo Championship Edition.  + Controls are precise and tight with a XBOX Controller. + Colors are vibrant. + Music is great. + Humor would give you a good chuckle. It doesn't tryhard to force you to laugh. + Combos are fun to smack the enemies around. + Has fast travel. Talking statues take you to one place to another instantly. + Spikes or pits won't insta-kill you unless it's the red saws. Red saws take you back to the checkpoint. + You can co-op with 3 other people, but I have no idea how this works because I have no friends. +- Juan is clear while the background is constantly out of focus. This might be a me problem, but it makes my eyes tired. Can be a problematic for others if they see it. +- The game has two endings. Alternate ending requires you to find 6 glowing orbs. One or two particular orbs will leave you with stress inducing rage. Hint: Don't break the barrels because they contain health and you may need them when doing the platforming. +- Some enemies can be a real pain in the butt, especially when they have a colored shield. - No voice acting which is a shame. It would have been perfect for Guacamelee. It's like watching a silent film.  Score: 9 out of 10. I found it to be fun regardless of the flaws it has.

没想到打完困难难度总的时间竟然达到了36小时。普通难度全收集完成了29/30个成就，花了大概28个小时。二周目困难难度没有刻意收集，体力和精力都不太够用，打起来也受了不少苦，8个小时通关。  总的来说是很有意思的一款古早类银，自认为有耐心的、喜欢连招的玩家可以一试。整体难度不难，但是17金牌挑战、5个隐藏Orb等内容有些关卡相当极限。点名Combo数那几关、两分钟扔鸡、闪烁切换连续跳跃那关等等。当然，后来发现这些关卡在特定道具[spoiler] （比如Combo挑战可以穿减普攻的衣服增加击中数、扔鸡挑战可以穿增加throw力量的衣服）[/spoiler]的帮助下难度降低不少，那是后话了。  要说最有意思的，我觉得还是[spoiler] 变鸡 [/spoiler]这个机制。兼具趣味性和实用性。  =============================================================== 游玩tips： 1. 对自己实力有信心、打算一周目全成就的可以直接作弊解锁困难模式。 2. 善用衣服。 3. 一些困难的地方可以直接油管搜Guacamelee的视频。包括挑战、收集等。 4. 关于Canal de les Flores 的地图完成度：[spoiler] 在中间部分，一个很像烟囱的空旷区域，有一个需要阴阳切换的隐藏房间，里面藏了三个东西，占整张地图完成度的15%。 [/spoiler]

The game stands out with its great climate and graphical style. I also appreciate the sense of humour even though occasionally it feels a bit forced. The metroidvania aspect of the game is simplified, but sometimes it is fun to play a more linear game, so I am not complaining about that. What I see as disadvantate is some aspects of combat: it is difficult to play without getting hit and there is this mechanics of falling without control after each hit (like in old Castlevania games) which is frustrating, because it makes combat less fluid; the game could also use more enemy variety, there is literally just a few types. On the other hand, I really liked the throwing mechanics- it is very fun to throw enemies around and observe the rest falling down like domino. When it comes to the bosses, they are uneven: some are decent, but the others seem uninspired. It is also the case with the level design- the world, even though its parts look different, is more or less the same with different looks. Platforming is usually fun, but can also get frustrating with getting through the spiky vines or troubles executing specific moves.  At the end of the game I had a reflection that the last stages are much better than the beginning. The reason is the access to all the powers and more challenging combat. All in all, a good game, but I must say that the Metacritic score seems exaggerated- maybe the standards got higher?

Guacamelee! es un aclamado videojuego de plataformas y acción desarrollado por DrinkBox Studios. Inspirado en la cultura mexicana, sigue a Juan Aguacate, un luchador que se embarca en una aventura para salvar a su amada y al mundo de las garras de Carlos Calaca. El juego combina un estilo visual vibrante, con gráficos coloridos y un diseño artístico que rinde homenaje al folclore mexicano, con una jugabilidad fluida que mezcla combates al estilo "beat 'em up" y exploración tipo metroidvania. Los jugadores utilizan ataques de lucha libre, como suplexes y golpes, mientras adquieren habilidades mágicas para desbloquear nuevas áreas. Su humor, referencias culturales y modo cooperativo lo hacen destacar.

Guacamelee Supercalifragilistic edition is the beefier version of the original game, now with some new fluff to fill out the game a little more.   If you've not played the original release, you can play this version perfectly fine as everything stays mostly the same. If you're a returning player, there's maybe an hour or so of new content which may or may not be worth your while.   Just to run things down for new players - Guacamelee is a Mexican Luchador Metroidvania where you traverse the realms of the living and the dead learning new moves and powers to chase down a skeleton that wishes to merge the two realms together. He also stole your lady.   Gameplay wise you're mainly beating up hoardes of enemies and platforming, progressively getting new moves and such to navigate and beat up bad guys. There's some new enemy types in this version and some alternate enemy versions too. Nothing major but it helps to flesh the game out a bit more.   The new ability is a lot of fun, it's basically a meter that fills up and lets you unleash a rage mode. It's rather fun to use but can trivialise some of the difficulty in the game when upgraded as you can absolutely wallop anything the game throws at you. There's also a couple of new areas and a new boss added in. Honestly they fit so well in that I felt I had just forgotten the areas were in the original. They're well placed and feel like they were always a part of the adventure.   The new and returning tracks all sound great and visually the game still pops with one of the new areas having a really nice aesthetic especially. I played this on deck and the colours really popped thanks to the OLED screen (It also runs great on deck btw)   All in all an upgrade to the original and a fun time throughout.

Don't expect a polished gameplay like other S-Tier Metroidvanias like Hollow Knight or Ori! This is the only reason I dropped the game before finishing it. I'll recommend it because Guacamelee is quite fun. The soundtrack is good and dialogues are funny, there's a really good sense of humor and I found myself giggling most of the time while playing. The story is kinda generic and I didn't care a single second about it, but hey you won't play this game expecting a deep and meaningful story, you're here only to wombo combo lucha libre style. Another good point is thtat you get skills and upgrades pretty early (and also pretty fast) throughout the game. But let's talk about the negatives, which isn't very much of a problem tbf.  The combat altough it's fun sometimes can get really - and I mean REALLY - stressful. I played on Keyboard (Razer Blackwidow, not some generic cheap brand) and the commands sometimes didn't respond. For instance: Sometimes you get the "E" to grab the enemy and the character won't or would turn it's back to the enemies out of nowhere. This is kinda janky but overall the enemies are so easy to defeat it won't even matter. Unless it happens in one of the many Arenas you have a countdown, then you'll die for ♥♥♥♥♥♥ coding and, yeah, it can be a little frustrating sometimes. On the bright side, there's a ton of save points and you shouldn't worry too much because you'll always die close to a checkpoint. This extends to the platforming and puzzles side of the game, for example: Sometimes you'll do a combo or double jump or anything that needs to be executed fast and the character won't do it as fast as you pressed because FPS, latency, cooldown or idk and honestly idrc also, because you'll get to repeat as many times needed and one time the game will register the inputs correctly and you'll get it done sooner or later. Just re-doing stuff sometimes can get extremely boring.  I dropped the game on the last boss because of this, but I had so much fun in the 6-ish hours I played it. Won't return to finish it tho, I have other (better) games to invest my hours. Wait a discount and buy it. 7/10.

Luchador masks, mystical realms, and bone-cracking combos – Guacamelee! Super Turbo Championship Edition doesn’t just step into the ring, it suplexes its way into one of the most vibrant and mechanically slick Metroidvanias out there.  From the moment you jump into the pixel-punching shoes of Juan Aguacate, a humble agave farmer turned supernatural wrestler, the world of Guacamelee grabs hold with color-soaked visuals and never really lets go. The art style oozes charm — Day of the Dead imagery dances with Mexican folklore in a way that feels both respectful and refreshingly original. Every environment feels handcrafted, with backgrounds bursting with hidden gags and cultural nods that are more delightful than distracting.  Combat, at its core, is the game’s beating heart. It's fast, fluid, and satisfyingly weighty. Punches hit like thunderclaps, throws send enemies crashing into walls with comic-book flair, and unlocking new abilities never stops feeling rewarding. Each new move you learn has a purpose — not just for pummeling foes but for exploring previously unreachable areas, creating a clever synergy between brawling and exploration. The addition of dimension-switching between the world of the living and the dead adds a beautiful twist to both traversal and puzzle-solving. It keeps the gameplay loop fresh and layered throughout.  But for every flaming uppercut, there’s the occasional stumble. Some platforming segments become a true test of patience — especially when they demand pixel-perfect jumps combined with mid-air dimension swaps and ability chaining. These can shift from challenging to frustrating pretty fast, breaking the otherwise joyful rhythm. And while the combat is engaging, certain enemy encounters can feel a bit too chaotic, especially when multiple shield types are thrown at you at once. It’s less “strategy” and more “survive the mess.”  Narratively, the game leans into humor with glee, often landing the jokes but occasionally overstuffing dialogue with self-aware quips that feel forced. Still, the charm usually wins out, particularly through its memorable characters and unexpected emotional beats that surface beneath the punchlines.  For returning players or those who played the original Guacamelee, this edition adds more areas, bosses, and gameplay polish. The enhancements feel meaningful rather than tacked on — a true evolution rather than just a repackaging.  Pros:  Exuberant art style that feels alive and rich with cultural flavor  Deep, combo-rich combat system that rewards mastery  Clever world design blending exploration with progression seamlessly  Memorable characters and laugh-out-loud moments  Strong replayability with secrets and hidden areas to uncover  Cons:  Some platforming sections verge on punishing, bordering on tedious  Late-game combat can feel like a sensory overload  Humor sometimes leans too heavily on internet-era references  Minor pacing dips during backtracking  Final Rating: 8.5/10 Guacamelee! Super Turbo Championship Edition delivers a spicy blend of action, humor, and worldbuilding that sticks the landing more often than not. It’s a fiesta of fists, with just enough kick to keep both newcomers and veterans hooked. Thank you!

Guacamelee Super Turbo es todo lo que fue la edicion previa pero ahora con contenido extra que lo hace ser más entretenido aún. Aunque sea el "turbo" su caracteristica mas destacable a primeras, tambien incluye nuevas zonas que se sienten bastante más trabajadas que algunos espacios de la obra pasada y nuevos enemigos que hacen el juego un poco más dificil que antes (y también más entretenido). Si bien todo esto bien podria ser un DLC sobre el juego original, pero creo que es más bien por la forma de inclusión en la historia que lo hace ser algo aparte. Dado un punto, en el transcurso de la historia es que ocurre el cambio de lo que fue el juego original a lo que es esta entrega, por lo que la transición es fluida hacia el nuevo contenido. Sin embargo creo que se siente un poco este cambio por el aumento de calidad y nivel de detalles que luego tienen algunos escenarios. Finalmente creo que Guacamelee Super Turbo Edition es una version mejorada y lo que debio haber sido el Guacamelee original, por lo que si no has jugado la versión Gold, juega esta únicamente.

《墨西哥英雄大混战:超级漩涡冠军版》个人评价【10/10】 【故事剧情】-【紧张刺激】 故事以卡洛斯·卡拉卡绑架了总统的女儿，并计划以她为祭品，将生者世界与死者世界合二为一，而作为青梅竹马色主角胡安，为了救出公主带上摔角手面具，踏上充满危险的征程。 【上手难度】-【手残噩梦】 作为类银河恶魔城的横版格斗游戏，难度是数一数二的，对手残党非常不友好，尤其在面具碎片的收集和全金牌的难度系数非常高，很容易砸坏手柄。 【视觉画面】-【风景如画】 游戏整体以二维画风为主体，多样而富有细节的世界，包含热闹的城镇、黑暗的森林、花香四溢的水道和火山岩浆。 【音乐音效】-【身临其境】 不同区域不同的悠扬的音乐，以及在战斗中激情的战斗使得游玩体验上升。 【玩法机制】-【独树一帆】 游戏主要以不同招式公鸡升龙拳、羚羊冲撞、爆气等组合打连击，以及道路上的各种小解谜，并且游戏支持双人游玩。 【优化适配】-【中规中矩】 游戏优化还是可以的，但是玩的过程中有很小的概率闪退。

Guacamelee traînait depuis un bon moment dans mon backlog, et je suis vraiment content d’avoir enfin pris le temps de m’y plonger. J’ai beaucoup apprécié l’expérience, même si je n’ai pas poussé jusqu’aux modes de difficulté supérieurs pour débloquer la "vraie" fin.  Le gameplay est particulièrement bien maîtrisé. J’ai pris beaucoup de plaisir à enchaîner les adversaires en combinant les différentes compétences mises à disposition. Chaque nouvel outil enrichit les possibilités de combat sans alourdir la prise en main, ce qui rend le tout à la fois dynamique et satisfaisant.  Graphiquement, le jeu est coloré et a du caractère, mais j’ai trouvé le style un peu trop anguleux par moments. Un peu plus de finesse dans les détails n’aurait pas été de trop. Les couleurs très vives aident à bien distinguer les compétences, mais nuisent légèrement à la cohérence visuelle globale.  En mode normal, la difficulté est globalement accessible. Quelques combats vers la fin proposent un peu plus de résistance, mais rien de vraiment bloquant : même le boss final ne m’a pas posé trop de problèmes.  Là où Guacamelee brille vraiment, c’est dans ses phases de plateforme et de puzzle. C’est ingénieux, bien rythmé, et surtout, les compétences sont utilisées de manière créative, ce qui évite toute impression de répétition.

한글패치 제작했습니다. https://gall.dcinside.com/indiegame/204123         (아래는 한패 제작 전 작성했던 리뷰)     [h3] 멕시코 배경의 콤보액션&플랫포밍 메트로배니아 [/h3]  [b] 평가: 갓겜 [/b] 조작: 키보드 난이도 옵션: 초회차(보통), 싱글 플레이 플레이 타임: 17.3시간 도전과제: 26/30   메트로배니아 장르의 입문작으로 뭐가 좋은지 고민되곤 했는데 이 게임이야말로 딱인 것 같다. 게임성, 만듦새, 난이도, 편의성, 정통성 모든 면에서 장르 입문작으로 딱 맞는 작품.   멕시코 망자의 날을 배경으로 하고 루차도르(프로레슬러)가 주인공인데 컨셉만큼 게임의 개성이 강함. 원색이 강조된 각진 그래픽, 멕시코 전통 음악, 유머러스하고 엉뚱한 대사와 컷신 등으로 밝고 왁자지껄한 분위기가 게임을 관통한다. 선배 게임들에 대한 패러디는 덤.  장르 내 타 게임들에 비교했을 때 난이도가 꽤나 쉬운 편임. 피격 데미지가 약하고, 플랫포밍도 대부분 간단하며, 세이브 포인트도 많아서 엔딩을 보기 매우 수월한 편이었다. 게임 진행이 선형적이고 목적지 안내도 있어서 길찾기 스트레스도 전혀 없었음. 그렇다고 응애 난이도라는 건 아니고 보스전과 일부 플랫포밍 등 꽤나 도전적인 구간들도 있어서 딱 가성비 있게 성취감 느끼기 좋은 난이도로 느껴졌다. (참고로 시작화면에서 코나미 커맨드 치면 초회차도 HARD 난이도 플레이 할 수 있다고 함. 그러면 초회차에 올도과 가능. 올도과 꼭 해야하는 게 아니라면 개인적으로는 비추.)  액션 시스템이 독특한 편. 메트로배니아로서는 흔치 않게 맨손격투 액션게임이다. 따라서 잡고 던지는 공격도 있고, 콤보도 있고, 에너지 게이지도 있음. 순서대로 스폰되는 적들을 격파해야 다음을 진행할 수 있는 스테이지형 구간도 자주 등장함. 그래서 아빠와 나 같은 벨트스크롤 액션게임이 떠오르기도 한다. 수월한 난이도의 전투이긴 하지만 여러 커맨드 때문에 손 꼬이는 시스템이긴 해서 다소 호불호 갈릴 수는 있겠음. 화려한 그래픽 탓에 피아구별이 힘든 것도 그렇고.  플랫포밍은 벽차기, 각종 대쉬, 레이어 전환 등 익숙한 맛이었는데 쉬워도 맛있는 훌륭한 완성도였다. 오리, 할나, 더메신저 같은 게임들이 떠오르는 요소들이 있지만 사실 얘가 먼저 나온 게임임. 늦게 플레이한 탓에 다소 익숙하게 느껴지긴 해도 워낙 고퀄이라 재밌게 했음.  탐험 면에서는 길찾기는 쉬웠지만 맵간 유기성이 좋고 재방문 동기부여도 잘 되어 있어서 탐험하는 맛이 없지 않았음. 그리고 오래된 게임 치고 맵 편의성이 무척 좋았다. 맵의 윤곽이 구체적이고 점진적으로 밝혀져서 안 간 곳을 구분하기 쉬웠음. 맵마커가 없기는 했지만 어빌리티 게이트가 맵에 워낙 잘 표시되어서 오히려 편했음. 다만 텔레포트 장소가 너무 제한적인 건 다소 불편하긴 했다.   아쉬웠던 점을 꼽자면 먼저 난이도 곡선. 전투나 플랫포밍에서 어느 순간 갑자기 난이도가 급격히 오른다는 느낌이 들었다. 만약 입문작으로 플레이했다면 갑자기 막혀서 당황했을 것 같음. 그리고 장르에 익숙한 입장에서도 인페르노 챌린지는 너무 갑자기 매웠다. 본편에서 아껴둔 보따리 풀어놓은 느낌이라 재밌게 플레이하긴 했지만 게임의 균형 면에서 갑자기 너무 큰 분량이 툭 튀어나와서 어색했던 것도 사실임. 개인적으로는 진엔딩 조건과 별개의 챌린지로 넣어 놨으면 더 좋았을 것 같다.  그리고 스킨 시스템. 희귀재화로 스킨을 구매할 수 있는데 각각 다른 기능들이 붙어있음. 그런데 게임 내 재화 수량이 모든 스킨을 사기에 턱 없이 부족함;; 겉모양만 보고 고르다가 기능 좋은 스킨 못 살 수도 있다. 많이 아쉬운 부분.   몇몇 아쉬운 점은 있었지만 [b] 그래픽, 액션, 플랫포밍, 편의성, 최적화 등 거의 모든 면에서 완성도가 워낙 훌륭했던 게임이었다. 장르 입문작으로 강추.
