Guacamelee Super Turbo Championship Edition Review  Review is after completing the main story missions and a few of the initial side quests. [hr][/hr]   [b]Story[/b] The story is about Juan (a simple Mexican agave farmer) and his quest to rescue his crush El Presidente’s daughter from an evil undead Charro. The story is simple but the main attraction of the game is its humor and its colorful art style. The game has no voice acting; only having dialogue prompts, but that dialogue is also laced with good humor. Along the way in his rescue his crush Juan meets a bunch of helpful characters who teach him the moves and powers required to become a luchador. It turns out that Juan must become a fully fledged luchador to save his beloved. He also meets a cast of enemies who are also similarly hilarious. Overall it was an enjoyable story that was wonderfully inspired by Mexican culture.  The only complaint I have is regarding the ending of the game. The game requires you to find about 6 hidden items in order to get the good ending. This only becomes apparent after playing the game and receiving the bad ending. The game doesn’t tell you that you need to find all these hidden areas (in addition to the hidden chests), at least I don't remember any prompts or hints about these hidden areas. Thankfully a good auto save point just before the final boss battle allows players to backtrack through the maps, collect the hidden items and thus get the good ending. I did not fulfill this task as I did not have time to backtrack.   [b]Gameplay[/b] This is my first Metroidvania style game and so I played on Normal difficulty. The gameplay was very entertaining because it had a healthy mix of platforming and combat. Platforming and exploration required creative use of the various luchador powers as well as excellent timing. Because of this getting to some hidden chests required a lot of retries and button mashing. I was not able to get to all the hidden chests but I managed to get at about 75% of them. The combat was very satisfying, supported by a great enemy variety. The enemies are also colorful and have a variety of attacks. The luchador costumes were also very amusing in their functionality and they are unlockable after earning special coins through gameplay: as it should be! As I remember when it comes to backtracking, all the enemies did not respawn; which is so refreshing, because I was dreading the grind. But fortunately only some of the enemies respawned or was it the enemies I skipped? I don't honestly remember. But either way backtracking wasn’t a slog. Out of all the powers I think Pollo power is unmatched. I won’t spoil what that is but it matches the game’s humor and themes very well.  As mentioned I did not backtrack to find all the hidden items required to get the good ending of the game. I tried out one of those hidden areas and got the hidden items but backtracking felt tedious. I understand that one of the draws of Metroidvania games is backtracking to previous areas to collect the hitherto inaccessible areas or show those tough bosses who’s the hero of the story. But this is not my choice so I did not backtrack much; I found it tedious after a while. The only annoying part of platforming were the portals. There were some parts at the end where quick reflexes were needed to move forward through the portals. I found this tedious and unenjoyable compared to all the other excellent platforming sections.   [b]Graphics[/b] The art style was colorful and lovely and subsequently the graphics were also vibrant and refreshing to look at. This is one of those game that will look good regardless of whether you have a low range or mid range graphics card. Backgrounds, enemies, bosses and allies have the same lively color theme. An absolute treat to look at.   [b]Controls[/b] Played using the Xbox controller attached to the PC. A controller is mandatory for this game. Some button mashing was required at times and at times attacks did not register correctly. So there were some frustrations but they did not prevent me from finishing the game.    [b]Side Missions[/b] There are a few side missions in this game. I was only able to complete a handful of these. For most of them I couldn’t find where to go to complete them so they were simply forgotten as I progressed.   [b]Music/Soundtrack/Voice[/b] The game has no voice acting which I did not find to be a negative. The soundtrack was thematic and adequate and all the funny sounds accompanying power ups encounters and combat filled out the gameplay experience nicely!   [b]Performance[/b] Experienced no crashes and the game ran smoothly on my PC (which is a mid-range PC). It did not cause undue heating of the CPU and GPU.   Overall Score 8/10

Perfect Game #26, Guacamelee! STCE.  A great blend of some of my favorite genres.  It sounds a little crazy to say that a platform-fighter metroidvania adventure game would work, but Guacamelee pulls it off fantastically.  The combat is fluid and there is a lot of depth to the system.  The world is filled with secrets and is really fun to explore as well.  The areas are detailed and filled with easter eggs of other games.  The characters are very funny, but can be a little over the top at times.  Overall it's definitely worth checking out, even for the combat alone.

Game-play is great. The combat system is super responsive, and generally easy to learn. The combos can be pretty technical, but you don't have to learn the complex ones to get through the game, and you can usually stick to your own bread-and-butter combos for the majority of your fights. The art style is amazing, and the soundtrack is great. The platforming is arguably more difficult than the combat at some points, and there are certain parts that have the potential to make you rage. It will likely take you at least 5 hours to beat the game for your first time, and even if you speed-run, it will still take you a couple of hours to beat. However, to COMPLETE the game in its entirety, (all side-quests/achievements) it will probably take tens of hours. Replayability is also good, I have played through the game about 6 times and still enjoy it. Overall, the game is great and I would recommend it to anyone looking for a platform fighter.

Ленивые суши "Филадельфия" Ингредиенты:  рис – 200 гр; вода – 225 гр; рисовый уксус – 20 гр; нори с васаби – 1 упаковка; красная слабосоленая рыба; творожный сыр авокадо или/и огурец. Способ приготовления:  Рис промыть 2-3 раза. Отправить его в посуду с толстым дном, залить водой, накрыть крышкой и поставить на сильный огонь. Как только вода закипит, уменьшить огонь до минимума и варить 12 минут. Крышку ни в коем случае не открывать. После выключить огонь, накрыть кастрюлю полотенцем и оставить рис еще на 15 минут. Можно сварить рис в мультиварке на режиме "крупа".  Готовый рис заправить рисовым уксусом, добавить мелконарезанные чипсы нори. На кусочек пищевой пленки выложить красную рыбу, рис, слегка придавить, в центр положить сыр и кусочек авокадо.   Готовить - это просто =)

I wasn't planning on playing this until it was chosen as the Game of the Month for August 2023. I like metroidvanias and I figured why not give it a try.  What I liked: * The colours and the music. It's surprisingly beautiful. * The platforming sections get more frustrating towards the end but the game is short enough to suffer through some moments. I liked that falling off the stage wasn't instant death. * Fighting with the different coloured shields takes some getting used to, but it's a fun mechanic. * Some other mechanics are introduced that make battles interesting to say the least. * One of your forms is hilarious to me. * There are some easter eggs of different games (especially the song in the final dungeon ha!) that makes progressing fun to find the next thing. * Boss fights are fun enough and easy to figure out.  What I didn't like: * The story was not too interesting and the ending was abrupt to say the least. * Falling off the stage is fine, when you fall into lava your transported back to the save point... * Combat gets messy when there are a lot of enemies in the area. Your fall animation is painful because it takes a long time. There's one particular punchy enemy that drove me crazy because of the loop of falling and him beating me I would get stuck in.  It was a fun experience and I can recommend this game for people who liked the mechanics in the Messenger but who would like a little bit less challenge.

Drinkbox games is one of the best at these kinds of 2d metroidvania platformers.  In fact, I think the platforming in their games is better than anything in Hollow Knight, and about on par with the Ori games. I particularly love the difficulty tuning.  The game keeps throwing new enemies, new mechanics, new traversal methods and areas at you but it never feels overwhelming because the difficulty curve rises at just the right pace.  The difficulty always feels just right, keeping some pressure on your but without abrupt spikes, just right to maximize fun.  I can't praise most of the game too highly enough with the sole exception, sadly, of the last phase of the last boss Calaca.  He's very big, too big for all of him to fit on the screen at one time, which make the tells for his attacks kind of hard to read sometimes.  Jaguar and Flame Face are both more fun to fight imho.

En abril de 2013 se lanzaba este juego para la PlayStation 3 y la PS Vita,  recuerdo que me gustó mucho el arte conceptual y el diseño del mapa y ya que en aquel entonces no tenía una consola lo jugaba en el almacén de un centro comercial cada que podía. Años después este juego se convirtió en el primero que pirateé en la primera computadora que llevaron a casa, nunca me lo pasé ya que me daba pereza ejecutar los combos con teclado XD.  Sim embargo, ya con la oportunidad de comprarlo y jugarlo puedo decir que este indie es sumamente bueno. Tiene un muy buen diseño de niveles, la verticalidad de sus mapas es adictivo y la simplicidad del combate es una de sus mejores cartas de presentación. La variedad de enemigos y jefes es muy decente, cuando crees que ya no verás más arquetipos de enemigos te sueltan uno nuevo acorde a la temática del mapa que te hará variar la forma en que has estado combatiendo, haciendo mucho más dinámico el gameplay y aportando a tus skills. Los obstáculos dentro de cada mapa son igual de retadores, no son nada del otro mundo pero sí que te harán pensar un poco y te retarán a mejorar/coordinar el timing de pulsaciones de tu teclado y mando. El "único defecto" que puedo ponerle a este juego es la simpleza de un par de combates con jefes, fuera de eso todo en cuanto al gameplay está correcto. Y, además, contiene una buena parte de secretos aún por descubrir y pequeñas misiones secundarias en los poblados de algunos mapas e incluso desafíos de habilidad.  De la historia, música y diseño artístico tengo que mencionar que son exquisitos. En tema de trama no esperes algo presentado a la altura de los metroidvanias más de culto pero sí que es una historia entrañable con un final que sí te hace sentir un vacío, más aún con la última imagen después de los créditos.  Este juego es muy recomendable, me hace querer sacarle todos los logros y darle otra pasada en la dificultad difícil. Después de 10 años de su salida estoy contento por haber concluido su historia. Recomendado.
